tossups.question: In this novel, a man recalls listening to hellfire sermons as a boy in Kansas while caring for a woman vomiting blood from self-starvation as they hide in a grain silo. After traveling with an avant-garde filmmaker from Yemen to Jordan, this novel’s protagonist is horrified to learn that “jebel” means “hill” in Arabic. In this novel, a woman who at one point attacks her husband with a potato peeler takes a job at a museum in British Columbia, further alienating the father from his son Tap. In this novel, a group of globetrotting American investors including Dot and Dick Borden and Lindsey and David Keller constantly worry about the (*) Iran Hostage Crisis. In this novel, a cult that kills victims based on their initials and the towns that they are in is investigated by the language-obsessed archaeologist Owen Brademas and the risk analyst John Axton, who moves from the U.S. to Athens. In 2023, the Library of America republished this anthropology-themed novel alongside the politically-themed Libra and the sociologically-themed White Noise. For 10 points, name this 1982 Don Delillo novel titled for a group of designations.

tossups.question: A year after moving to the U.S., this man, under the umbrella of the organization ICF, founded a series of international scientific conferences, one of which was chaired by Nobel laureate John Eccles. This man promoted the idea that humans are restored to God’s ideal by doing good things via the process of “indemnity.” This man, who wrote a 1966 religious text called Divine Principle, called his family the “True Family” and was known to followers as the “True Father.” The prologue to Don DeLillo’s novel Mao II describes a “blessing ceremony” presided over by this man at Yankee Stadium. Adherents of this man’s movement sold flowers in airports and engaged in mass wedding ceremonies. For 10 points, name this founder of the Unification Church who was a South Korean religious leader.
tossups.answer_sanitized: Sun Myung Moon [or Mun Seon-myeong; or Mun Yong-myeong]

tossups.question: One character in this novel is aggrieved when his building's gimpy superintendent accuses him of having stolen two snow shovels, which he did in fact steal; later, those shovels are abandoned in a bar by Antoine because there is no room for them in his garbage-filled car. The protagonist of this novel rents a Lexus to visit an art installation in the desert that consists of 230 decommissioned Air Force planes. A video recording made by a child of a man driving a Dodge and being shot recurs in this novel; at one point, anchorwoman Sue Ann Corcoran receives a phone call on-air from the shooter, the "Texas Highway Killer". In the opening section of this novel, Jackie Gleason eats too many hot dogs and vomits on Frank Sinatra's shoes, while a reprint in Life magazine of Bruegel's Triumph of Death mesmerizes J. Edgar Hoover. Cotter Martin acquires the baseball from the "Shot Heard 'Round the World" in the first section of, for 10 points, what 1997 novel about waste management executive Nick Shay by Don DeLillo?

This novel is characterised by its rotating cast of protagonists, who include Richard, the Highway Killer, and a man whose adult conformity hides the spontaneity with which he killed a man as a teenager, Nick Shay. For 10 points, identify this novel by Don DeLillo, centred on the symbols of the baseball and the radioactive core of a nuclear bomb.

tossups.question: A character in this novel explains that if she heard the phrase "speeding bullet," she would duck for cover. Another character in this novel plans to break the world record for longest time spent in a cage of venomous snakes. This novel's protagonist argues with his son over the metaphysics of whether it is raining. In this novel, Murray Jay Siskind hopes to create a course on Elvis at the College-on-the-Hill. In this novel, the experimental drug Dylar reveals an affair between Willie Mink and the protagonist's fourth wife, Babette. A derailed train in this novel releases a black cloud of Nyodene D into the air, an event known as "The Airborne Toxic Event." For 10 points, name this novel about Jack Gladney, written by Don DeLillo.

bonuses.leadin: In a notable scene from this film, the protagonist hallucinates that his torso contains a VCR. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this horror film in which Max Renn discovers a broadcast of people being tortured and killed in an orange room. The broadcast actually causes brain tumors.
This Canadian director of Videodrome also directed a film about diseased organic game consoles called Existenz. He also directed Scanners, a remake of The Fly, and Eastern Promises.
David Cronenberg wrote, produced, and directed this 2012 film based on a Don DeLillo novel. In this film, currency speculator Eric Packer sits in his limo worrying about his asymmetric prostate.

parts: Name this Don Delillo novel in which non-Germanophone Hitler Studies professor Jack Gladney is exposed to chemicals during the Airborne Toxic Event. It also describes his wife Babette taking Dylar to cure her fear of death.

An anonymous man watches 24 Hour Psycho at the beginning and end of this latest DeLillo novel, in which the poetry-loving ex-Department of Defense scholar Richard Elster retires to a desert to contemplate deep time.
DeLillo’s novel Falling Man, named for a performance artist who leaps out of buildings, opens with this event. Keith Neudecker, who survives this event, has a son who fears the imaginary Bill Lawton.

bonuses.leadin: Answer these questions about postmodern American literature, for 10 points each.
parts: In this Don Delillo novel, Hitler Studies professor Jack Gladney learns that his wife Babette has been cheating on him to obtain Dylar and obsesses over his impending death after coming into contact with the Airborne Toxic Event.

This author included ludicrous features like the Peter Pequid society, a hyper right-wing organization that opposes capitalism because it leads to communism, in The Crying of Lot 49. This reclusive author also wrote V. and Mason and Dixon.
This John Barth novel concerns Billy Bockfuss, a man raised as a farm animal, who is trained by the giant computer WESCAC to become the messianic Grand Tutor of New Tammany College.
answers: Giles Goat-Boy [or The Revised New Syllabus of George Giles our Grand Tutor]

bonuses.leadin: The protagonist of this novel is convinced that he will die soon after he is exposed to Nyodene D during an “Airborne Toxic Event,” while his wife becomes addicted to the drug Dylar. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this novel, in which professor Jack Gladney eventually loses his fear of dying after watching his son Wilder successfully ride his bike across a highway.

In Don DeLillo’s White Noise, Jack is a professor in a field dedicated to this historical figure. The DeLillo novel Running Dog is about the search for a pornographic film reportedly created by this man.

Speaking of Hitler, this contemporary of DeLillo imagined the highly antisemitic Charles Lindbergh winning the election of 1940 in his novel The Plot Against America. More well known novels by this man include American Pastoral and Portnoy’s Complaint.
